paper_metadata.title: Big Bird: Transformers for Longer Sequences
paper_metadata.abstract: Transformers-based models, such as BERT, have been one of the most successful deep learning models for NLP. Unfortunately, one of their core limitations is the quadratic dependency (mainly in terms of memory) on the sequence length due to their full attention mechanism. To remedy this, we propose, BigBird, a sparse attention mechanism that reduces this quadratic dependency to linear. We show that BigBird is a universal approximator of sequence functions and is Turing complete, thereby preserving these properties of the quadratic, full attention model. Along the way, our theoretical analysis reveals some of the benefits of having $O(1)$ global tokens (such as CLS), that attend to the entire sequence as part of the sparse attention mechanism. The proposed sparse attention can handle sequences of length up to 8x of what was previously possible using similar hardware. As a consequence of the capability to handle longer context, BigBird drastically improves performance on various NLP tasks such as question answering and summarization. We also propose novel applications to genomics data.

extraction_data.innovation: BIGBIRD introduces sparse attention mechanisms (random, sliding window, and global tokens) to reduce quadratic complexity while maintaining expressive power. It combines random, localized, and global attention to achieve linear complexity and improved performance on long sequences.
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Not specified in the paper; assumed to be general language tasks
extraction_data.optimizer: Not mentioned in the paper; assumed to be Adam or similar (common for transformer models)
extraction_data.extension: The BIGBIRD model incorporates global tokens to capture long-range dependencies and locality, extending the original transformer architecture with theoretical guarantees on expressiveness and computational efficiency.
extraction_data.research_problem: Handling longer sequences in Transformers without quadratic memory/computation costs
extraction_data.application: Long sequence modeling, natural language processing, and computational biology
extraction_data.paper_title: Big Bird: Transformers for Longer Sequences

extraction_data.innovation: BigBird introduces sparse attention mechanisms to handle long sequences efficiently, enabling the model to process sequences longer than typical Transformers. It uses a sparse attention mechanism to reduce computational cost while maintaining accuracy.
extraction_data.application: Long sequence processing, natural language processing, sequence-to-sequence tasks
extraction_data.extension: Sparse attention mechanism to reduce computational cost for long sequences
extraction_data.paper_title: Big Bird: Transformers for Longer Sequences

extraction_data.innovation: BIGBIRD extends Transformer architecture with block diagonal attention to handle longer sequences, enabling efficient and effective modeling of long-range dependencies.
extraction_data.application: Long-sequence sequence modeling, genomic analysis, and other long-context tasks
extraction_data.research_problem: Handling long sequences in sequence modeling
extraction_data.paper_title: Big Bird: Transformers for Longer Sequences
